Name: Trends: interval=month → 200
Description: Interval=month should return 200.

Name: Run checks: quality score is 0–100
Description: Quality score in the response must be a number between 0 and 100 inclusive.

Name: List datasets: authenticated → 200 with structure
Description: Authenticated GET /api/datasets should return 200 with a datasets array and total count.

Name: Update: no auth → 401
Description: PATCH without a token should return 401.

Name: Upload: valid CSV → 201 with metadata
Description: Uploading a valid CSV should return 201 with correct row/column counts and PENDING status.

Name: Report JSON: passed + failed = total_rules
Description: passed_rules + failed_rules must equal total_rules — rule counts must be consistent.

Name: Create: invalid severity → 400
Description: An invalid severity value (e.g. CRITICAL) should return 400.

Name: Run checks: valid dataset + rule → 200
Description: Running checks on a dataset with a matching rule should return 200.

Name: Get results: each item has required fields
Description: Each result item must contain rule_id, passed, failed_rows, and total_rows.

Name: Login: wrong password → 401
Description: Providing an incorrect password should return 401 Unauthorized.

Name: Login: empty body → 422
Description: An empty JSON body for login should return 422.

Name: Get results: another user → 403
Description: A user should not be able to view check results belonging to another user's dataset.

Name: Report JSON: has all required fields
Description: Report JSON must contain score, total_rules, passed_rules, failed_rules, and results.

Name: List datasets: user isolation — second user cannot see first user's datasets
Description: A regular user should not be able to see datasets uploaded by another user.

Name: List rules: authenticated → 200 with list
Description: Authenticated GET /api/rules should return 200 with an array of rules.

Name: Register: password has no letter → 422
Description: Password with only digits and no letters must be rejected with 422.

Name: Report JSON: 200 with correct content type
Description: Fetching a report for a validated dataset should return 200 with application/json content type.

Name: Register: weak passwords (parameterized) → 422 [2] Weak password: no letter at all
Description: Various weak passwords should all be rejected with 422.

Name: Report CSV: body contains data
Description: The CSV body must be non-empty and contain at least Score or Rule headers.

Name: Update: deactivate rule → 200
Description: The rule owner should be able to deactivate a rule by setting is_active=false.

Name: Report JSON: results list is non-empty
Description: The results list in the report must contain at least one entry.

Name: Create: UNIQUE rule → 201
Description: UNIQUE rule requires no parameters and should return 201.

Name: Trends: interval=day → 200
Description: Interval=day should return 200.

Name: Update: invalid severity → 400
Description: Updating a rule with an invalid severity should return 400.

Name: Delete: another user's rule → 403
Description: A user should not be able to delete another user's rule.

Name: Delete: non-existent rule → 404
Description: Deleting a non-existent rule ID should return 404.

Name: Create: RANGE rule with min/max → 201
Description: RANGE rule with valid min and max parameters should return 201.

Name: Report: another user's dataset → 403
Description: A user should not be able to view another user's dataset report.

Name: Upload: response contains all required schema fields
Description: Upload response must include id, name, file_type, row_count, column_count, and status fields.

Name: Login: missing password → 422
Description: Login request missing the password field should return 422.

Name: Login: valid credentials → 200 + token
Description: Valid credentials should return 200 with a JWT bearer token.

Name: Run checks: re-run is idempotent → 200
Description: Re-running checks on an already validated dataset should still return 200 with VALIDATED status (idempotent).

Name: Create: DATA_TYPE rule with params → 201
Description: DATA_TYPE rule with a valid expected_type parameter should return 201.

Name: Register: weak passwords (parameterized) → 422 [3] Weak password: no digit at all
Description: Various weak passwords should all be rejected with 422.

Name: Trends: filter by dataset_id → 200
Description: Filtering trends by dataset_id should return 200.

Name: Trends: interval=week → 200
Description: Interval=week should return 200.

Name: Register: duplicate email → 400
Description: Registering with an email that already exists should return 400 with 'already registered' in the detail.

Name: List datasets: no auth → 401
Description: GET /api/datasets without a token should return 401.

Name: Create: NOT_NULL rule → 201
Description: Creating a NOT_NULL rule should return 201 with is_active=true and correct fields.

Name: List rules: created rule appears in list
Description: The rule created in setUp should appear in the list for the owning user.

Name: Get results: after run → 200 with list
Description: After running checks, GET /api/checks/results/{id} should return 200 with a non-empty list.

Name: Register: missing full_name → 422
Description: Request body missing full_name should return 422.

Name: Create: no auth → 401
Description: Creating a rule without an Authorization header should return 401.

Name: Report: dataset not found → 404
Description: Requesting a report for a non-existent dataset should return 404.

Name: Upload: malformed binary CSV → 400
Description: Uploading a binary/corrupt file disguised as CSV should return 400.

Name: Run checks: another user's dataset → 403
Description: A user should not be able to run checks on another user's dataset.

Name: Get results: no auth → 401
Description: GET /api/checks/results without a token should return 401.

Name: Report: no auth → 401
Description: GET /api/reports without a token should return 401.

Name: Create: DATA_TYPE without params → 400
Description: DATA_TYPE rule without the required parameters field should return 400.

Name: Create: REGEX rule with pattern → 201
Description: REGEX rule with a valid regex pattern parameter should return 201.

Name: Register: missing email → 422
Description: Request body missing the email field should return 422.

Name: List rules: user isolation → second user cannot see first user's rules
Description: A second user should not see rules created by the primary user.

Name: Report CSV: Content-Disposition attachment header present
Description: CSV response must include a Content-Disposition header indicating an attachment download.

Name: Report JSON: score is valid (0–100)
Description: The quality score in the report must be a number between 0 and 100.

Name: Update: non-existent rule → 404
Description: Attempting to update a rule that does not exist should return 404.

Name: Update: another user's rule → 403
Description: A user should not be able to update a rule they do not own.

Name: Login: non-existent user → 401
Description: Attempting to log in with a non-existent account should return 401.

Name: List datasets: uploaded dataset appears in list
Description: The dataset that was just uploaded should appear in the list response.

Name: Register: valid user → 201 + JWT token
Description: A new user with a valid email and strong password should receive a 201 response containing a JWT bearer token.

Name: List datasets: skip=999 → empty list
Description: Using a skip value larger than the total dataset count should return an empty list.

Name: Delete: deleted rule disappears from list
Description: After deletion, the rule should no longer appear in the list.

Name: Trends: days=7 → 200
Description: Using days=7 filter should return 200.

Name: Register: weak passwords (parameterized) → 422 [1] Weak password: too short (under 8 chars)
Description: Various weak passwords should all be rejected with 422.

Name: Create: RANGE min >= max → 400
Description: RANGE rule where min is greater than or equal to max should return 400.

Name: Trends: invalid interval → 400
Description: An unrecognised interval value should return 400.

Name: Create: invalid rule_type → 400
Description: An unrecognised rule_type should return 400.

Name: Run checks: dataset not found → 404
Description: Running checks on a non-existent dataset ID should return 404.

Name: Run checks: no auth → 401
Description: POST /api/checks/run without an Authorization header should return 401.

Name: Report CSV: 200 with text/csv
Description: Requesting the report with ?format=csv should return 200 with text/csv content type.

Name: Upload: valid JSON → 201
Description: Uploading a valid JSON array file should return 201 with json file_type.

Name: Delete: no auth → 401
Description: DELETE without a token should return 401.

Name: Delete: owner deletes rule → 204
Description: The rule owner should be able to soft-delete their rule, returning 204 No Content.

Name: Update: change name → 200
Description: The rule owner should be able to update the rule's name.

Name: Register: empty body → 422
Description: An empty JSON body should return 422.

Name: Trends: no auth → 401
Description: GET /api/reports/trends without a token should return 401.

Name: Run checks: response has all expected fields
Description: The check result must contain dataset_id, status=VALIDATED, score, results_summary, and execution_time_seconds.

Name: Create: empty field_name → 400
Description: An empty or whitespace-only field_name should return 400.

Name: Upload: .txt file → 400 unsupported type
Description: Uploading a .txt file should be rejected with 400 — only CSV and JSON are supported.

Name: Upload: no auth → 401
Description: Upload request without an Authorization header should return 401.

Name: Trends: default request → 200
Description: Default GET /api/reports/trends should return 200.

Name: Get results: dataset not found → 404
Description: Getting results for a non-existent dataset should return 404.

Name: Upload: empty file → 400
Description: Uploading a zero-byte file should be rejected with 400 and an 'empty' error detail.

Name: List rules: no auth → 401
Description: GET /api/rules without a token should return 401.

Name: Upload: invalid token → 401
Description: An expired or tampered JWT token should be rejected with 401.

Name: Upload: headers-only CSV → 400
Description: A CSV file containing only headers and no data rows should return 400.

Name: Update: change severity → 200
Description: The rule owner should be able to change the severity.

Name: Register: password has no number → 422
Description: Password with only letters and no digits must be rejected with 422.

Name: Register: password too short → 422
Description: Password shorter than 8 characters must be rejected with 422.
